filing_context.section_description: Credit/Debit Notes issued to registered recipients
filing_context.data_granularity: One row represents one credit/debit note line entry. A single note may appear in multiple rows if different tax rates or taxable values are reported separately.
filing_context.purpose: Give an AI system a structural understanding of how this filing file is arranged, without relying on real transaction data.

file_structure.number_format_note: Numeric values may appear as plain numbers or as comma-formatted strings such as '5,887'. They should be normalized before computation.

columns.description: GSTIN or UIN of the registered recipient to whom the credit/debit note relates.
columns.validation_hint: Usually a 15-character GSTIN/UIN-like identifier. Keep as text, not number.

columns.description: Legal or trade name of the recipient/customer.
Credit note or debit note document number.
columns.validation_hint: Keep as text to preserve prefixes, leading zeroes, and special characters.
Common format in the file is D-MMM-YY or D-MMM-YYYY, e.g. '1-Apr-18'.

columns.description: Identifies whether the document is a credit note or debit note.
columns.validation_hint: C = Credit Note, D = Debit Note.

columns.description: State code and state name of place of supply.
columns.validation_hint: Usually stored as '<state_code>-<state_name>'. Keep as text.

columns.description: Nature/category of supply for the note.
columns.validation_hint: Treat as controlled text but allow portal-specific variants.

columns.description: Total value of the credit/debit note.
columns.validation_hint: Normalize comma-formatted values and blanks before numeric processing.

columns.description: Applicable percentage of tax rate, if relevant for the transaction.
columns.validation_hint: Often blank in portal templates unless specifically applicable.

columns.description: GST tax rate applicable to the taxable line.
columns.validation_hint: Expected as a numeric percentage rate. Keep flexible because exported files may contain unusual or legacy values.

columns.description: Taxable amount on which the tax rate is applied.
columns.validation_hint: May appear with comma separators in CSV exports; convert to decimal for calculations.
Blank should be interpreted as zero only during computation, not necessarily in source preservation.

do: Treat the first row as the exact header structure.
Preserve GSTIN/UIN, Note Number, and Place Of Supply as strings.
Normalize dates into a standard internal date format only after preserving the source text.
Normalize numeric fields by removing commas and converting blanks where necessary.
Allow multiple rows for the same Note Number if tax rates or line values differ.
do_not: Do not infer missing tax values from placeholders.
Do not treat blank Applicable % of Tax Rate as an error by default.
Do not remove rows only because Cess Amount is blank.
Do not convert GSTIN/UIN or Note Number into numeric values.

minimum_validation_rules: Column names and column order should match the template.
Required fields should not be blank: GSTIN/UIN of Recipient, Receiver Name, Note Number, Note Date, Note Type, Place Of Supply, Reverse Charge, Note Supply Type, Note Value, Rate, Taxable Value.
Note Type should normally be C or D.
Reverse Charge should normally be Y or N.
Note Value, Rate, Taxable Value, Applicable % of Tax Rate, and Cess Amount should be numeric when not blank.
Date values should be parseable from the source date format.
